grader_proofs_verified: In the end, Oron defeated Shahak and Rahab prevailed over Tarshish as the Hebrew Language Academy on Wednesday unveiled the new names of two planets referred to until now by non-Hebrew names.
Henceforth, Uranus will be known as "Oron" and Neptune as "Rahab."

The vote, which was conducted via the web, was jointly managed by the university unit and the Hebrew Language Academy under the title "A Hebrew Star is Born" - a play on the title "A Star is Born," Israel's version of the popular television show "American Idol."
Nearly 3,000 voters preferred "Rahab" for Neptune, more than double those who wished to see it named Tarshish.

Because the proof exists in the source yet was not among the shown sentences, this is a tool display problem, not an author problem.
fable_reasoning: Retrieval miss: the source proves the web vote managed by the Hebrew Language Academy, the 2009 timing, the Biblical sea-monster origin, and Rahab as the official Hebrew name for Neptune. Only 'Book of Psalms' is specified where the source says 'Bible and Talmud', a benign detail -> supported (arbiter concurred).
